task_completion.description: Agent explores the source code directory to identify device drivers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window or file manager is open showing the contents of /opt/openice/mdpnp or its subdirectories.

task_completion.description: Agent opens the OpenICE Supervisor application to check for simulated adapters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenICE Supervisor graphical user interface (GUI) is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Agent creates the required report file on the Desktop.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'device_driver_audit.txt' (or an icon for this file is visible on the Desktop).

task_completion.description: The report lists at least 4 distinct medical device manufacturers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file 'device_driver_audit.txt' contains entries for at least 4 different manufacturers (e.g., Philips, Masimo, Dräger, Nellcor, Nonin, etc.).

task_completion.description: The report includes the directory path to the driver code for each manufacturer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file shows a 'Path:' entry for each manufacturer, pointing to a location within /opt/openice/mdpnp/.

task_completion.description: The report includes the simulation availability status for each manufacturer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text file shows a 'Simulation Available: Yes' or 'Simulation Available: No' entry for each listed manufacturer.

integrity.description: Agent actually opened the OpenICE Supervisor application to check simulation availability.
integrity.visual_evidence: The OpenICE Supervisor GUI must be visible in at least one screenshot, proving the agent didn't just guess or hallucinate the simulation availability.

integrity.description: Agent actually explored the source code directory.
integrity.visual_evidence: A terminal or file manager showing the /opt/openice/mdpnp directory contents must be visible, proving the agent didn't just generate the file from prior knowledge.

integrity.description: Agent listed real manufacturers from the repository, avoiding known metadata errors.
integrity.visual_evidence: The listed manufacturers in the text file are genuine (e.g., Philips, Masimo, Dräger, Nellcor, Nonin, Oridion, CPC, Fluke, GE Healthcare, Mindray, Covidien, Puritan Bennett) and do NOT include 'ivy' or 'orion' (which are metadata errors/hallucinations).

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected report path is /home/ga/Desktop/device_driver_audit.txt. The source code is located at /opt/openice/mdpnp. The report must list at least 4 distinct manufacturers. Valid manufacturers with drivers in the repository include: Philips, Masimo, Dräger, Nellcor, Nonin, Oridion, CPC, Fluke, GE Healthcare, Mindray, Covidien, and Puritan Bennett. Note: 'ivy' and 'orion' are NOT valid manufacturers in this repository and their inclusion indicates hallucination or cheating.
